nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary or sole research tool
nodes.grounding_span: It's more like a stepping stone when I've really run out of my own ideas

nodes.label: AI as metaphor generator for abstract concepts
nodes.grounding_span: It's often more useful for finding better or more idiomatic ways to say things in English

nodes.label: AI rigidity on definitions
nodes.grounding_span: It's really rigid on specific definitions in some ways and tends to be pretty resistant to outside-the-box interpretations

nodes.label: AI for language translation
nodes.label_negative: Lack of AI integration in translation tools
nodes.grounding_span: Trados has basically no useful AI integration at all

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool
nodes.label_negative: AI causing loss of creativity and lowering standards
nodes.grounding_span: as long as that reduces the workload for 'busywork', that's fine. I think the issue is if it starts causing people in general to lose creativity and lower the bar

nodes.label: Autonomy and self-reliance
nodes.grounding_span: In general I prefer to handle everything myself unless it seems extremely onerous

nodes.label: Accurate understanding and representation
nodes.grounding_span: I of course verify anything the AI gives me

nodes.label: Professional efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI-powered QA seems like a complete no-brainer, yet nothing exists

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for terminology breakthroughs
nodes.grounding_span: That was something I was able to uncover with AI after working on this particular piece for over a decade

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: It's really rigid on specific definitions in some ways and tends to be pretty resistant to outside-the-box interpretations

nodes.label: Frustration with poor AI integration in Trados
nodes.grounding_span: I was extremely frustrated to discover that it seems that Trados has basically no useful AI integration at all

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's fairly inevitable that it's going to play a bigger role in everyone's work over time, and as long as that reduces the workload for 'busywork', that's fine

nodes.label: Self-reliant problem-solving with AI as last resort
nodes.grounding_span: In general I prefer to handle everything myself unless it seems extremely onerous

nodes.label: Verification-oriented approach
nodes.grounding_span: I of course verify anything the AI gives me
